description: One scheduled payment entry within a PaymentSchedule: an agreed amount due on an event or a fixed date, carried through the maker-approves / checker-releases / maker-processes pipeline. Its status (null=pending | APPROVED | PARTIAL_APPROVAL | PROCESSED | RESCHEDULED) drives the schedule lock, the paid/upcoming/overdue breakdown, and the overdue reminder. A partial approval (amount < agreed) creates exactly one remainder installment; the first reschedule captures the original date (axiom A9).

fields.name: identifier
fields.label: Installment Ref.
fields.validation: configurable pattern; unique; immutable once created
fields.purpose: Stable key for the entry so approval, processing, reschedule, and remainder linkage all resolve to one installment and the audit trail traces each disbursement leg.

fields.name: scheduleId
fields.label: Payment Schedule
fields.validation: PaymentSchedule exists; read-only after creation
fields.purpose: Links the entry to its owning schedule so the schedule total, the lock guard, and the breakdown aggregate across all sibling installments.

fields.name: amount
fields.label: Agreed Amount
fields.validation: > 0; stored as Float (null treated as 0); editable only while the entry is null/RESCHEDULED and the schedule is unlocked
fields.purpose: The agreed value this installment is meant to settle; the ceiling an approval cannot exceed and the basis for the leftover remainder when an approval is partial. The agreed figure against which paid/upcoming/overdue are measured.

fields.name: dueEvent
fields.label: Due Event
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the entry is tied to an event rather than a fixed date; mutually exclusive with date (one of event OR date is required)
fields.validation: one of the event values; provide an event OR a fixed date, not neither
fields.purpose: Expresses an event-driven due trigger (after PO, against documents, on delivery, on milestone acceptance) so the installment becomes due when the procurement event occurs rather than on a calendar date.

fields.name: date
fields.label: Due Date
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the entry is tied to a fixed date rather than an event; mutually exclusive with dueEvent
fields.validation: valid date; a null date is treated as upcoming (never overdue) in the amount breakdown; set to newDate on reschedule
fields.purpose: The calendar due date that drives the overdue-vs-upcoming split (boundary 00:00:00 start-of-today) and the ~28-day overdue reminder. A null date is deliberately treated as upcoming so an undated entry never falsely shows overdue.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.validation: non-empty; copied unchanged to the remainder on partial approval
fields.purpose: The human-readable label for the entry (e.g. advance against documents, balance on delivery) shown on the schedule and the payment confirmation so each leg is identifiable.

fields.name: term
fields.label: Payment Term
fields.validation: one of the locked PaymentTerms list; no free-text terms
fields.purpose: The commercial term this entry derives from, kept on the line so the reminder lead (about 28 days before a 30/60/90-day term) and the due calculation read the term per installment.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: null(pending) | APPROVED | PARTIAL_APPROVAL | PROCESSED | RESCHEDULED; null->APPROVED (amount==agreed); null->PARTIAL_APPROVAL (0<amount<agreed, +one remainder); APPROVED/PARTIAL_APPROVAL->PROCESSED (terminal, no un-process); null/PROCESSED->RESCHEDULED
fields.purpose: The lifecycle position that gates every action: it determines processability (only APPROVED/PARTIAL_APPROVAL process), drives the schedule lock (any APPROVED/PARTIAL_APPROVAL/PROCESSED locks), and classifies the entry in the paid/upcoming/overdue breakdown. null means pending, not absent.

fields.name: approvedAmount
fields.label: Approved Amount
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when status in {APPROVED, PARTIAL_APPROVAL, PROCESSED}
fields.validation: > 0 AND <= agreed amount, else BadRequestException and the entry stays pending; equals agreed on full approval
fields.purpose: The amount the maker approves to pay; the basis of the net-release netting and the sum that counts toward Paid once PROCESSED. When less than agreed it triggers the single remainder for the leftover.

fields.name: remainderInstallmentId
fields.label: Remainder Installment
fields.mandatoryCondition: set on the parent when a partial approval created a remainder
fields.validation: exactly one remainder per partial approval (axiom A9); remainder has null status, leftover = agreed minus approved, same date/schedule/description
fields.purpose: Links the parent entry to the one remainder created when an approval is partial so the leftover (agreed minus approved) re-enters the next-entry loop as a fresh pending installment. Exactly one remainder per partial, never more.

fields.name: approvedById
fields.label: Approved By
fields.validation: the maker who approved; recorded with approvedAt
fields.purpose: Records who approved the installment amount for the audit trail and the maker/checker segregation (the approver is the maker, distinct from the checker who releases).

fields.name: approvedAt
fields.label: Approved At
fields.validation: set on approval; immutable thereafter
fields.purpose: Timestamps the approval for the audit trail and to order the approval-to-process timeline of the entry.

fields.name: approvalNote
fields.label: Approval Remarks
fields.validation: free text
fields.purpose: Optional note the maker records at approval (e.g. why a partial amount) for the audit narrative.

fields.name: processedAt
fields.label: Processed At
fields.validation: set on process; immutable; PROCESSED is terminal
fields.purpose: Timestamps the actual disbursement; the payment-date anchor for realized FX gain/loss and for DPO. Marks the entry as settled in the Paid total.

fields.name: processedById
fields.label: Processed By
fields.validation: the maker who processed; recorded with processedAt
fields.purpose: Records who processed the disbursement for the audit trail; the maker processes after the checker releases, never the checker.

fields.name: paymentDate
fields.label: Payment Date
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when status == PROCESSED (entered on process)
fields.validation: valid date; entered on the process step
fields.purpose: The effective date money left the account; used for the payment-date FX rate (realized gain/loss between PO and payment) and for cash-flow and DPO reporting.

fields.name: processNotes
fields.label: Process Notes
fields.validation: free text
fields.purpose: Optional note captured at processing (e.g. bank reference) for the audit narrative.

fields.name: receiptFile
fields.label: Payment Receipt
fields.validation: optional on process; stored at receipts/{ticketId}/{originalFilename}; an upload failure fails the whole process operation so status is never set without the intended receipt
fields.purpose: The proof-of-payment document attached at processing; the evidence an auditor inspects to confirm the disbursement. Its upload failing aborts the process so a PROCESSED entry is never left without its intended receipt.

fields.name: originalDate
fields.label: Original Due Date
fields.mandatoryCondition: set on the first reschedule only
fields.validation: captured from the current date on the first reschedule; preserved unchanged on every later reschedule
fields.computedFormula: = date at the moment of first reschedule
fields.purpose: Preserves the originally promised due date the first time an entry is rescheduled so the reschedule history is visible and the original commitment is never lost even after repeated holds. Captured once, never overwritten.

fields.name: rescheduleReason
fields.label: Reschedule Reason
fields.validation: free text; the hold reason (signatory unavailable / cash flow)
fields.purpose: Records why an entry was rescheduled (the checker hold reason) so the deferral is explained in the audit trail rather than appearing as a silent date change.

fields.name: overdueReminderSent
fields.label: Overdue Reminder Sent
fields.validation: set true when the ~28-day-before-due reminder fires; informational only, never changes installment status
fields.purpose: Tracks that the overdue payment reminder (fired about 28 days before a 30-day term is due, same day-count lead for 60/90) has been emailed to Accounts so the maker prepares the due credit installment. Informational only; it never advances the status.

invariants: A9 partial approval (amount < agreed) creates exactly one remainder; schedule locked if any installment in {APPROVED, PARTIAL_APPROVAL, PROCESSED}
A6 SoD maker approves/processes, checker releases; maker != checker
A13 audit + SSE (INSTALLMENT_STATUS_UPDATED) on every committed change
